summary: Linked Popo's neutral-special file `ftPp_SpecialN.c` at 100% match, replacing the assembly object with the C object and deleting `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPopo/ftPp_SpecialN.s`. The C implementation completed the previously `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` static callback `ftPp_SpecialN_8011F500`, added required headers/prototypes, and tightened item-related typing for Popo fighter vars and `it_27CF` functions.
what_changed: Changed `configure.py` so `melee/ft/chara/ftPopo/ftPp_SpecialN.c` is built as `Matching` instead of `NonMatching`.
Changed `obj_files.mk` to use `src/melee/ft/chara/ftPopo/ftPp_SpecialN.c.o` instead of the deleted assembly object `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPopo/ftPp_SpecialN.s.o`.
Implemented `ftPp_SpecialN_8011F500`, which handles Popo neutral-special command-variable cases for spawning or releasing the associated item object and playing related fighter effects/sounds.
Added C includes needed by the linked implementation, including `ftPp_Init.h`, `ft/ft_0877.h`, `ft/ft_0881.h`, `it/forward.h`, and `lb/lb_00B0.h`.
Changed the internal callback declaration and definition from `HSD_GObj*` to `Fighter_GObj*`.
Changed Popo fighter var `x222C` from `uint` to `Item_GObj*`, matching its observed use as an item object pointer.

Updated `it_802C1590` prototype from a one-argument void function to `Item_GObj* it_802C1590(Item_GObj*, Vec3*, enum_t, float facing_dir)` and changed `it_802C17DC` to take `Item_GObj*` instead of `uint`.
Changed `ftPp_Init_8011F16C` parameter naming/type from `uint` to `Item_GObj* item_gobj`, with the comparison now against `fp->fv.nn.x222C` as an item pointer.

Updated `config/GALE01/scratches.txt`; the diff shows a large same-size churn around scratch records and adds OK entries around the Popo/Ice Climber address range, but the excerpt does not establish any review rationale for the scratch changes.
smart_moves: Propagated the item-pointer realization consistently through fighter vars, item headers, and Popo init callback signatures instead of only casting locally in the matched function.

Used `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` and a `Fighter_GObj*` signature in the completed callback, aligning the implementation with fighter code conventions and avoiding opaque `HSD_GObj*` handling where the object is known to be a fighter.
Introduced a small inline cleanup helper, `inlineA0`, to match repeated assembly behavior that clears `x222C`, `death2_cb`, and `take_dmg_cb` only when the tracked item pointer still matches.

Preserved exact matching details in `ftPp_SpecialN_8011F500`, including `PAD_STACK(4 * 2)`, temporary `Vec3 pos`, command-variable branching, null checks, and distinct effect calls for Popo vs non-Popo fighter kind.
Set `death2_cb` and `take_dmg_cb` to `ftPp_Init_8011F060` only when item creation succeeds, matching the assembly's conditional callback installation.
decomp_lessons: When a fighter var stores an object handle, promote the field from integer-like storage to the correct `*_GObj*` type and then repair all dependent prototypes; this often unlocks cleaner matching and safer callsites.

For fighter special-state callbacks, `cmd_vars[0]` may encode animation-event commands such as spawn/release actions; structure the C control flow around explicit command values when the assembly compares against small constants.
Item ownership cleanup must verify pointer identity before clearing fighter callbacks and item references; do not blindly null callbacks if the item pointer may have changed.
Object-file linkage PRs should update both the Python build configuration and `obj_files.mk` so the C object replaces the asm object everywhere.
Small `PAD_STACK` additions can be part of the required match when a local vector's stack layout needs to mirror the original assembly.
Even when the functional implementation is small, adjacent header typing changes can be the important reusable work because they reduce future placeholder casts.
naming_conventions: Special move lifecycle functions use the established fighter suffixes `Enter`, `Anim`, `IASA`, `Phys`, and `Coll`.
Air/ground variants are distinguished as `SpecialN` and `SpecialAirN`.
The unresolved/internal callback retained the address-style name `ftPp_SpecialN_8011F500`; the PR does not provide a semantic rename.
`Item_GObj* item_gobj` is the preferred spelling for item object parameters in the touched declarations.
Existing unknown fighter vars remain offset-style names such as `x222C` and `x2250`; this PR only improved type information, not semantic field names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced the assembly file after a report showing `ftPp_SpecialN.c` at 1.17 KB with every listed function at `100.00%` and score `0`.

Matched the spawn branch by reading a joint position with `lb_8000B1CC(fp->parts[0].joint, NULL, &pos)`, offsetting it with `ftIceClimberAttributes` fields `xC` and `x10`, and spawning an item via `it_802C1590(gobj, &pos, 106, fp->facing_dir)`.
Matched command `1` by storing the returned item object in `fp->fv.pp.x222C`, playing `ft_80088148(fp, 130021, 127, 64)`, setting death/damage callbacks if non-null, and clearing `cmd_vars[0]`.

Matched command `2` by checking `x222C`, calling `it_802C16F8`, clearing `cmd_vars[0]`, playing one of two `ft_800881D8` IDs depending on `fp->kind == FTKIND_POPO`, then playing `ft_80088148(fp, 130024, 127, 64)`.
Used `NULL` comparisons and pointer equality in C to reproduce assembly `cmplwi`/`cmplw` checks around the item object.

Added an empty inline `inlineA1(Item_GObj* item_gobj, Fighter* fp) {}` in the diff; the excerpt does not show its functional role, so it should be treated as a matching artifact rather than inferred behavior.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that semantically rename `ftPp_SpecialN_8011F500`, `x222C`, or `x2250`.
Check whether Nana's corresponding neutral-special implementation reuses the same item pointer cleanup and callback patterns.
Search for other `it_802C1590` callsites to confirm the item spawn prototype and item kind argument naming.
Investigate later cleanup of `config/GALE01/scratches.txt` churn around Popo/Ice Climber scratch entries.
